findings.issue: Task 5 (wave 2) declares depends_on: [1, 2, 3] but AppModule in this task binds DefaultChatRepository (created by Task 4, also wave 2). The task's own Details.Dependencies section acknowledges 'Task 4 must also be complete since AppModule binds DefaultChatRepository', yet task 4 is absent from depends_on. Two wave-2 tasks cannot run in parallel when one produces an artifact the other consumes.
findings.fix: Add 4 to depends_on: [1, 2, 3, 4]. Then consider moving task 5 to wave 3 to keep wave 2 containing only tasks that can run in parallel (tasks 1-4 all independent; task 5 assembles them).

findings.issue: Task 8 declares wave: 4 and depends_on: [6, 7]. Task 7 is also wave 4. The wave invariant requires that a task's wave must be strictly greater than the wave of every task it depends on. Task 8 depending on task 7 (wave 4) means task 8 must be wave 5 or higher.
findings.fix: Set wave: 5 on task 8. This also cascades: task 9 depends_on: [7, 8] and is currently wave 5 — it must move to wave 6. Adjust task 8 → wave 5, task 9 → wave 6 (and downstream tasks 10, 11 shift accordingly). Alternatively, remove task 7 from task 8's depends_on if task 8 can genuinely start before task 7 is complete — but the nav args (chatId, kind) introduced by task 7 are required by task 8's SavedStateHandle reading, so the dependency is real.

findings.issue: Required Skills section lists two skills: /skill:code-writing and /skill:project-knowledge. Frontmatter skills only declares [code-writing]. The frontmatter and body are inconsistent — project-knowledge appears in the body but not in the skills array.
findings.fix: Either add 'project-knowledge' to the frontmatter skills array (skills: [code-writing, project-knowledge]) and ensure the corresponding reviewer mapping is valid, or remove /skill:project-knowledge from the Required Skills section if it is not a formal skill for this task (project-knowledge is a reference skill, not a code-writing reviewer-gated skill). Check patterns.md for the correct skills↔reviewers mapping.

findings.issue: Reviewer log paths contain the unfilled template placeholder {round}: 'code-reviewer-{round}.json' and 'test-reviewer-{round}.json'. These are literal strings in the file, not resolved values.
findings.fix: Replace {round} with the starting round number 1: 'code-reviewer-1.json', 'test-reviewer-1.json'.

findings.issue: Reviewer log paths contain the unfilled template placeholder {round}: 'code-reviewer-{round}.json', 'security-auditor-{round}.json', 'test-reviewer-{round}.json'.

Task 6 asserts 'SettingsMigrationHelper is already provided by AppModule from Task 5', but Task 5's AppModule specification does not include a @Provides entry for SettingsMigrationHelper. SettingsMigrationHelper is a concrete @Singleton class created in Task 2 — Hilt resolves it via constructor injection automatically without an explicit @Provides in AppModule. The claim that Task 5 provides it in AppModule is inaccurate and will mislead the implementing agent into looking for a @Provides that does not exist.
findings.fix: Correct the statement: 'SettingsMigrationHelper is a @Singleton @Inject constructor class (created in Task 2); Hilt resolves it automatically via constructor injection — no explicit @Provides in AppModule is needed.'

findings.issue: Task 9 declares wave: 5 and depends_on: [7, 8]. Task 8 is declared at wave 4 but should be wave 5 (per finding on task 8). If task 8 is corrected to wave 5, task 9 must move to wave 6 to maintain the wave invariant. This is a cascading consequence of the task 8 wave error.
findings.fix: After fixing task 8 to wave 5, set task 9 to wave: 6. Also verify downstream tasks 10 and 11 (not in this batch) to ensure their waves are updated consistently.

findings.issue: Context Files for project knowledge use relative paths: '../../.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/project.md' and '../../.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/architecture.md'. From the task file location (work/phase-3-history/tasks/8.md), two levels up is work/phase-3-history/ — not the project root. The correct relative path would be '../../../.claude/...' (three levels up). Other tasks in this batch (5, 9) use absolute Windows paths for PK files, which is more reliable.
findings.fix: Replace relative PK paths with absolute paths matching the pattern used in tasks 5 and 9: 'C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/project.md' etc. Similarly fix 'ChatViewModel.kt', 'ChatRepository.kt', 'WarmupCoordinator.kt', 'DefaultModelRegistry.kt' links which use '../../../app/...' — from tasks/ directory '../../../' resolves to work/ not PhoneWrap/; correct to '../../app/...' or use absolute paths.

findings.issue: Required Skills path uses tilde-relative notation '~/.claude/skills/code-writing/SKILL.md' while tasks 5 and 9 in this batch use absolute Windows paths. This is an inconsistency — tilde paths may not resolve correctly on Windows.
findings.fix: Replace with the absolute path: 'C:/Users/Vladimir/.claude/skills/code-writing/SKILL.md' to match the pattern in tasks 5, 7, 9.

findings.issue: Reviewer log paths use hardcoded round number '1' (e.g., 'code-reviewer-1.json'). This is consistent with other fixed-round tasks in batch 1, so it is acceptable, but the template allows for {round} as a variable. Minor format note — not a blocker.
findings.fix: No change required if the team convention is to use '1' as the starting round. If the convention uses {round}, update accordingly.

findings.issue: Reviewer log paths use hardcoded round number '1'. Same minor note as task 5 — consistent with batch 1 convention but does not match the template's {round} variable pattern.
findings.fix: No change required if '1' is the convention for first review round.

findings.issue: The ChatScreen stub guidance says 'you may keep a temporary TODO("Task 8 reworks ChatScreen signature") inside the new chat/... composable lambdas'. This is a TODO marker — per checklist, TODO markers are flagged.
findings.fix: Rephrase as an instruction without the TODO marker: 'If ChatScreen signature is not yet compatible, use a placeholder Text composable in the chat route lambda stubs until Task 8 updates the signature.'
